topic: A neighbor asks to use part of the yard for a garden
raw_output:
[story 1]

The afternoon sun was warm on my neck as I was watering my prize-winning petunias when Mrs. Henderson shuffled over, clutching a slightly wilted trowel. "Excuse me, Robert," she began, her voice a little shaky. "I was wondering if I might possibly borrow a section of your lawn for a little vegetable patch? I've got some heirloom tomatoes I'm really keen on growing." I froze, the hose suddenly feeling heavy in my hand. My mouth went dry. I blinked several times, trying to process the request. It was just a yard. A patch of grass. Why was this suddenly an insurmountable hurdle? I managed a weak, "Uh, well, I suppose..." My brain felt like scrambled eggs.

[story 2]

I was meticulously pruning my rose bushes, the scent of damp earth filling the air, when the doorbell rang. It was Mr. Davies, my neighbor, looking earnest and slightly flustered. He stood on my porch, holding a folded piece of paper. "Hello, Mark," he said, adjusting his glasses. "I have a rather big idea for the unused corner of your yard. I was hoping you might consider letting me use that space for a small herb garden. It would be wonderful." I dropped my shears. The metal clattered onto the paving stones. My jaw felt suddenly slack. I stared at him, my eyes wide, unable to form a coherent response. All I could manage was a slow, deliberate nod, my gaze fixed somewhere past his shoulder.

[story 3]

I was just finishing up a tedious repair on my fence when Sarah from down the street appeared, carrying a watering can. "Hi," she chirped, stepping onto my property. "I saw that patch of open ground near the oak tree. Would you mind terribly if I set up a small garden there? I’m trying to learn about composting, and I think that spot would be perfect." I dropped the wrench I was holding. It hit the ground with a dull thud. My breath hitched in my chest. My hands started to tremble slightly. I could only stare at the ground between my feet, my mind utterly blank, unable to recall a single appropriate reply.
